vulnerability.reason: describes technical sensitivity from compute fluctuations but misses the social condition of resource-limited populations and the widening of digital divides
vulnerability.reference: Resource-limited populations depend on edge devices, so accuracy fluctuations widen existing digital divides.
vulnerability.generated: On-device ML models rely on one-shot subset selection that loses representativeness when subset size changes due to compute budget fluctuations.

impact.reason: affected party (marginalized users) and damage type (reinforcing inequalities) are absent; instead describes trust erosion and decision quality compromise
impact.reference: Marginalized users abandon unreliable ML services, reinforcing social and economic inequalities.
impact.generated: Degraded model accuracy and generalization, causing inconsistent application performance that erodes user trust and compromises decision quality.

dose_response.reason: covers technical causal links from compute fluctuations to accuracy loss but omits the final social outcome of user abandonment and deepening inequality
dose_response.reference: When fluctuating compute on edge devices leads to subset degradation, unrepresentative coresets cause accuracy drops, exposing resource-limited users to unreliable services they abandon, deepening inequality.
dose_response.generated: Subset selection methods producing unrepresentative datasets, through inconsistent on-device model accuracy, trigger representativeness loss from subset size changes due to compute budget fluctuations, leading to degraded model accuracy and generalization that erode user trust and compromise decision quality.
